skill.name: longbridge-content
skill.description: Analyze listed company news, filings, community topics, SEC EDGAR documents, and market regulatory rules using Longbridge data sources.
skill.summary: Analyze market news, filings, community topics, SEC reports, and trading rules with Longbridge data.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 66 external-command locations and 8 weak-cryptography blockers. Review found the weak-cryptography blockers are false positives in financial and regulatory prose, and most command detections are Markdown CLI examples. The skill still requires running the Longbridge CLI and allows a WebSearch fallback, so it should publish with a medium warning rather than be blocked.

medium_findings.title: External Longbridge CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to run the Longbridge CLI for news, filings, topics, market status, and SEC filing analysis. This is an intended capability, but it can execute local commands with user-provided symbols or keywords and retrieve external data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and repeated across the skill documentation. I found no shell interpolation or executable script, so the risk is operational rather than confirmed command injection.

medium_findings.title: Write-Capable Community Commands Are Documented
medium_findings.description: The topic reference includes create and create-reply subcommands even though the skill is presented as a read-oriented content skill. This could cause an agent to consider write actions if a user asks for community posting.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write-capable commands are present in CLI help text, but the skill usage examples only show read and search commands. This is a plausible misuse risk, not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Optional WebSearch Fallback
medium_findings.description: The news workflow allows WebSearch when Longbridge data is empty, stale, or missing a breaking event. This leaves the primary Longbridge data boundary and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fallback condition and disclosure text are explicit in the reference file. The risk is limited because the source must be labelled and there is no hidden endpoint.

low_findings.title: Static Shell-Execution Findings Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Most external-command detections are backticks, fenced code blocks, command names, or output templates in Markdown. They document intended CLI use and are not Ruby backtick execution or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are Markdown documentation, not Ruby or shell scripts. The flagged syntax appears inside prose, tables, or fenced examples.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography detections correspond to market terms, percentages, rule text, or SEC filing template language. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, key handling, or password handling was found at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain financial, regulatory, or output-template text. I did not find evidence of weak cryptographic algorithm use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on commands such as longbridge news, longbridge filing, longbridge topic, longbridge financial-report, and longbridge static. These commands should be treated as external tool execution and bounded to the documented Longbridge use cases.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly documented in command examples. The examples use fixed Longbridge subcommands, so the concern is permission and data boundary control.

content.value_statement: Market research requires current news, filings, community context, and rule interpretation from separate sources. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to organize Longbridge content into concise research summaries.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes recent stock news from Longbridge data.
Classifies news into catalyst, regulatory, strategic, financial, opinion, and other buckets.
Reviews company filings and SEC documents such as 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, proxy, and Form 4.
Reports community topic volume and coarse sentiment without quoting comments verbatim.
Explains selected A-share, Hong Kong, United States, and cryptocurrency market rules.
limitations: It depends on the Longbridge CLI, MCP tools, or a labelled WebSearch fallback.
It is not a licensed financial adviser and must not provide personalized investment advice.
Coverage can be sparse for smaller symbols or unsupported markets.
Regulatory rules can change and should be checked against official sources.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Equity News Brief
use_cases.description: Create a concise digest of recent news, filings, and community discussion for one listed company.

use_cases.title: Review SEC Filing Signals
use_cases.description: Extract financial trends, risk factors, management commentary, and event signals from recent SEC filings.

use_cases.title: Answer Market Rule Questions
use_cases.description: Explain trading limits, settlement rules, circuit breakers, margin rules, and related constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Get a Simple News Digest
prompt_templates.prompt: Use longbridge-content to summarize the latest Longbridge news for AAPL.US. Group the items by category and end with a neutral key takeaway.

prompt_templates.title: Compare News and Community Reaction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use longbridge-content to review NVDA.US news, filings, and community topics. Show the main facts, sentiment skew, and any missing data.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Recent SEC Filing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use longbridge-content to analyze the latest 10-Q for MSFT.US. Focus on revenue, margins, free cash flow, risk factors, MD&A, and investment signal.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Regulatory Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use longbridge-content to explain A-share price limits, Hong Kong settlement, and United States PDT rules. Include thresholds, consequences, and caveats.

output_examples.output: A categorized digest with recent Longbridge news, filings, community topic counts, sentiment skew, and a neutral key-facts section.

output_examples.input: Analyze the latest 8-K for a United States stock.
output_examples.output: A filing summary with form metadata, the material event, related news context, and a balanced investment signal.

output_examples.input: Can I day trade A-shares after buying today?
output_examples.output: A rule explanation covering T+1 stock settlement, limited T+0 exceptions, practical implications, and an official-source caveat.

best_practices: Ask for a precise symbol when the company name is ambiguous.
Label WebSearch results clearly when Longbridge data is missing or stale.
Keep conclusions fact-based and include the investment-advice disclaimer.
anti_patterns: Do not invent news, filings, prices, or regulatory details.
Do not quote individual community comments verbatim.
Do not use the topic create or create-reply commands for this read-oriented workflow.

faq.question: Does this skill provide investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes information and rules for reference only.

faq.question: Which data source does it prefer?
faq.answer: It prefers Longbridge data and platform capabilities.

faq.question: Can it analyze SEC filings?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports narrative analysis for 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, proxy, and Form 4 filings.

faq.question: Does it require login?
faq.answer: The skill states that its documented public commands do not require login.

faq.question: What happens when Longbridge data is empty?
faq.answer: It can use a clearly labelled WebSearch fallback when allowed by the workflow.

faq.question: Can it publish community posts?
faq.answer: No. Marketplace use should treat this skill as read-oriented research guidance.
